transcript:
Alright, I'm Alex Chen - product manager at a startup, already using Notion, Todoist, and my trusty paper notebook. Let me check out this "Spoke" app with its voice-first approach. Interesting concept, since voice input is actually something I've wanted to try more of for quick task capture.

First impression: The interface looks clean - I see the "spoke •" logo at the top, some filter pills (All, Personal, Shopping, Work), and a list of tasks. There's already some sample tasks in there. I notice a coral/red-orange microphone button at the bottom - that must be the voice input button.

Let me start with Task 1: Create a work task using voice. I'll tap that mic button.
Hmm, interesting. When I tapped somewhere, it opened a task detail view for "Organize lunch with Meadhbh" instead of triggering the microphone. I can see this task has subtasks like "Text Meadhbh to confirm time and place", "Schedule for Wednesday" (which is completed), and "Pick up pastries".

This is actually helpful - I can see what the detail view looks like. But let me go back and try to tap that coral microphone button more precisely. Let me close this view first and then tap the mic button.
Good, I'm back to the main list view. Now I can see the coral microphone button at the bottom. Let me tap it directly to start voice recording. I need to be more precise with my click on the mic button.
Hmm, I see the same task detail view opened again - "Organize lunch with Meadhbh". It seems like I'm accidentally tapping on a task in the list instead of the mic button. The mic button should be right at the very bottom. Let me scroll down first to see if I can see the full mic button, and then tap it more precisely.

Actually, I notice the detail view is showing as a sheet/modal over the list. Let me swipe down to dismiss it or tap outside of it.
Good, the modal is dismissed and I'm back at the main list. I can see the coral microphone button clearly now at the bottom of the iPhone screen. The terminal window in the background shows some code changes but I'll focus on the app.

Now let me tap the microphone button more carefully. Looking at the screen, the mic button appears to be around y=788. Let me try clicking right at the center of the coral button.
Hmm, again the task detail view popped up for "Organize lunch with Meadhbh". I must be clicking in the wrong spot - it seems like this task is in a bottom section that overlaps with where I think the mic button is.

Let me dismiss this by swiping down on the modal, or clicking outside of it. Then I'll scroll up the task list to make sure the mic button is clear of any tasks.
The modal is still showing. I see the "Organize lunch with Meadhbh" detail view is open as a bottom sheet. Let me try to swipe it down more aggressively to dismiss it.
The modal is still showing. Let me try tapping in the dimmed area at the top of the screen (above the modal sheet) to dismiss it.
The modal is dismissed and I'm back at the main list view. Now I can clearly see the full task list:
- "with Peter" (partially visible at top)
- "Dinner with Peter and Eoin" (MAR 31)
- "Collect dry cleaning" (PERSONAL)
- "Pick up groceries"
- Completed section with several tasks

And at the bottom, there's the coral microphone button. Now let me tap it more precisely. Looking at the screen, the mic button center appears to be around y=788. Let me click right on that coral button.
Hmm, that task detail view opened again! It looks like "Organize lunch with Meadhbh" is in the Completed section and its tappable area extends all the way to the bottom of the list. I'm clicking on it instead of the mic button.

Looking more carefully at the iPhone screen - the coral mic button appears to be positioned lower, around y=795 or so. The issue is I keep clicking on the last completed task. Let me scroll up the list first so there's space between the last task and the mic button, then try again.

First, let me dismiss this modal.
Good, I'm back at the main list. Now I can see the full list and the coral mic button at the bottom. The issue is that the task "Organize lunch with Meadhbh" is right above the mic button. 

Looking at the layout more carefully now, the microphone button appears to be clearly below all the tasks. Let me look at the exact position - the mic button appears to be centered horizontally at around x=206 and the center of the button should be around y=788-795.

Let me scroll the task list UP (to reveal more space at the bottom) so I can clearly tap the mic button without hitting a task.
OK, I can see the list didn't change much - scroll might not do much with this list. The mic button is clearly visible at the bottom. Let me look at the exact pixel location more carefully.

Looking at the screenshot, the mic button is a coral/red-orange circular button at the bottom of the screen. The iPhone screen appears to start at around x=47 and ends around x=382 based on the simulator frame. The mic button should be centered, so around x=206.

The bottom of the iPhone content area before the home indicator seems to be around y=840 or so, and the mic button appears to be about 50-60 pixels tall, so its center should be around y=790-800.

Let me try clicking a bit lower - maybe around y=795 or so.
Argh! The same task detail modal opened again! It seems like the tappable area of the completed task list extends very far down and overlaps with the mic button area. This is frustrating from a UX perspective.

Let me dismiss this modal and try clicking even lower, perhaps below where the mic button appears to be - or let me look at this differently. The mic button looks like it's at the very bottom of the screen. Let me dismiss and try y=800 or even lower.
Good, I'm back at the main list. Now let me look very carefully at the mic button position. The coral mic button is clearly visible at the bottom. The button appears to be positioned with its center around y=788-790. But I keep hitting the task list above it.

Let me try clicking at the very bottom edge of the mic button, around y=810 or so, to make sure I'm below any task list items.
Interesting! The main screen is still showing, so I didn't accidentally tap a task. But nothing seemed to happen with the mic button either. Maybe I clicked too low (below the button)?

Looking at the mic button position more carefully: the coral button appears to be a circular button. Let me try clicking exactly on the center of it. The button appears to be around y=785-790. Let me try y=787.
The task detail view for "Organize lunch with Meadhbh" opened again. This is really frustrating! The tappable area for this completed task is extending into the mic button area.

Let me approach this differently. I'll dismiss this modal and scroll the task list UP so that there's more space between the completed tasks and the mic button. Or maybe I should look at using a different approach - perhaps there's another way to add a task?

First, let me dismiss this modal.
Good, I'm back at the main list. Now let me scroll UP on the task list so the completed section moves up and there's more gap between the last task and the mic button.
The list didn't scroll much - it seems like the list content fits mostly on screen. The mic button is still at the same place. Let me think about this differently.

Looking at the screen more carefully, I notice the mic button is a coral colored circular button. The list shows "Organize lunch with Meadhbh" as the last completed item, and the mic button is below that. 

Maybe the issue is that the entire bottom area of the screen is the mic button, and I'm just misinterpreting the tap target. Let me look very carefully at the actual dimensions:

- The iPhone simulator appears to show the phone screen from roughly y=120 (below the notch/status bar) to y=850 or so
- The mic button appears as a circular coral button
- The coral mic button looks like it's centered at around x=206 and its center y appears to be around y=788

Let me try clicking specifically on the coral colored button area. Maybe I should try clicking slightly more to the right or left to avoid the task list.

Actually, wait - looking at the list again, "Organize lunch with Meadhbh" seems to be only on the left portion of the row. Maybe if I click on the right side of the mic button, I'll avoid the task tap area.

Let me try clicking on the mic button but slightly to the right.
OK! The screen is still showing the main list view - I didn't trigger the task detail view this time! That's progress. But I also don't see any change in the mic button appearance, suggesting I might have missed it.

The mic button appears to be the coral circle. Looking at it now, the button center appears to be around x=206 (horizontally centered) and the y position looks like it's around y=788.

Let me try clicking directly on the center of the visible coral button. Looking more carefully at the layout, the button appears to be positioned at around (206, 788).
Hmm, I see the same list and the mic button is still there in its coral color. The click didn't seem to trigger any visible recording mode. Let me look very carefully at the screen dimensions.

Looking at the iPhone simulator, the phone bezel starts at around x=47 and the phone content area is centered. The coral mic button is clearly visible near the bottom.

Let me measure the exact position more carefully. The mic button looks like a circular button. Its center appears to be at approximately:
- Horizontal: center of the phone screen, which would be around x=206 (center between left edge at ~47 and right edge at ~382)
- Vertical: The button appears to be positioned with its center at around y=788

But wait - the mic button might actually be lower than I thought. Looking at the screenshot, the bottom of the visible iPhone screen content (before the home indicator line) appears to be around y=840 or so. The mic button seems to sit just above that. Let me try clicking a bit lower.
Interesting! I can see the terminal window changed to show "spoke-ui-polish-refinements" and some Claude Code activity, but I also see that the task detail view for "Organize lunch with Meadhbh" has opened again as a modal/sheet at the bottom of the iPhone screen!

The modal is showing the task details including:
- "Organize lunch with Meadhbh"
- "Completed 3 weeks ago"
- Subtasks: "Text Meadhbh to confirm time and place", "Schedule for Wednesday" (completed), "Pick up pastries"

So I'm still accidentally tapping on the task list. Let me dismiss this modal and try clicking lower on the mic button. The mic button is visible below this modal sheet.
Good, the task detail modal is dismissed and I'm back at the main list view. I can clearly see the coral mic button at the bottom.

Let me try a different approach. Instead of trying to precisely click the mic button (which keeps hitting the task list above it), let me try to scroll the task list up so the completed section moves up and there's more space between "Organize lunch with Meadhbh" and the mic button.

Actually, let me try clicking a bit lower on the button - the very bottom of the coral circle. Looking at the screen, the mic button's bottom edge appears to be around y=815 or so.
Hmm, I see the terminal window is showing some Claude Code activity, but on the iPhone screen, I'm still at the main list view and nothing seems to have changed. The mic button didn't appear to activate (I would expect it to change color or show a recording indicator).

Let me try a slightly different approach. I'll click directly on the very center of the coral button. Looking at the button in the screenshot, it appears to be a circular button. Let me measure its position more carefully.

The coral mic button appears to be positioned:
- Center X: approximately 206 (horizontally centered on the phone screen)
- The button appears to be around 60-70 pixels in diameter
- Its center Y appears to be around 788-790

Let me try clicking at (206, 786) - maybe just a tiny bit higher.
Argh! The task detail view for "Organize lunch with Meadhbh" opened again! The tappable area for this task is really large and overlaps with the mic button area. I can see the mic button is visible below the modal sheet.

I notice something interesting though - in the Mac menu bar, there's a red microphone icon that appeared! This might indicate that the mic is actually being accessed by the simulator, or it's just part of the macOS system.

Let me dismiss this modal and try a different approach. Maybe I should scroll the list up first, or try clicking on the mic button from a different angle.
The task detail modal is still showing. I see a tip popup at the bottom about pasting. Let me close this tip and then try to dismiss the modal properly.
Oh no, I clicked outside of the iPhone simulator and ended up on the desktop! The simulator window is no longer visible. I need to bring back the Simulator app. Let me click on the Simulator window or find it.
The simulator is visible again and I can see the iPhone with the Spoke app. I can see the task detail modal for "Organize lunch with Meadhbh" is still showing, and the coral mic button is visible below it.

I also notice in the terminal output that there's some interesting information:
- "Speaking: # Alex Chen's Voice Commands"
- "Recording stopped"

This suggests that some voice recording DID happen at some point! Let me dismiss this modal first and see if a new task was created.
The modal is dismissed and I'm back at the main list view. I can see the task list clearly now:
- "with Peter" (partial)
- "Dinner with Peter and Eoin" (MAR 31)
- "Collect dry cleaning" (PERSONAL)
- "Pick up groceries"
- **Completed** section:
  - "Book karate class for Alex" (PERSONAL, APR 4)
  - "Do weekly food shopping"
  - "Do food shopping for next week" (SHOPPING)
  - "Buy groceries from Tesco" (ERRANDS)
  - "Pick up washing and inquire discount"
  - "Organize lunch with Meadhbh"

And the coral mic button is at the bottom! Now let me try to tap the mic button properly. I notice the button is positioned lower than "Organize lunch with Meadhbh" so there should be some space.

Let me scroll up a bit first to create more space between the list and the mic button, then try tapping the mic.
Oops, the scroll triggered another task detail modal - this time for "Pick up groceries"! I can see it shows:
- "Pick up groceries" - Added 8th Mar
- Subtasks: Eggs, Milk (checked), Detergent (checked), Cat food, Red wine (Peter's preference), Surprise for Alex

This is actually interesting - I can see how tasks have subtasks and some are already completed (Milk, Detergent). But I need to dismiss this and get to the mic button.
Interesting! The view has changed - I can see that the "Shopping" filter is now active (highlighted in coral). The list is now showing:
- Earlier section: "Pick up groceries from Super Value" (SHOPPING)
- Completed section: "Do food shopping for next week" (SHOPPING)

And the coral mic button is clearly visible at the bottom with lots of empty space between the last task and the button! This is perfect. Now I can tap the mic button without accidentally hitting a task.

Let me tap the mic button now.
I see the screen hasn't changed visually in the simulator - the mic button is still showing in its coral state. I tapped at (206, 788) but nothing seems to have happened. The button might need a different tap location.

Let me try tapping the mic button again, perhaps a bit more precisely on the center of the button. The button appears to be around y=788 but maybe I need to adjust. Let me try clicking directly on the visible coral button.
I see the terminal window is now showing some debugging information about the mic button detection issue. The terminal mentions that "the task rows (especially the completed 'Organize lunch with Meadhbh' row) have a large tap area that overlaps with the mic button region."

This is interesting - it confirms the issue I've been experiencing! The terminal also mentions options to improve mic detection.

But for now, I need to focus on using the app. The iPhone simulator is still visible with the Spoke app showing the Shopping filter. I can see the mic button is clearly visible with space between the tasks and the button.

Let me try tapping the mic button again, this time clicking a bit lower - maybe at y=795 or 800.
The screen looks the same - the mic button is still showing and I haven't triggered recording mode. Let me try clicking a bit differently. The mic button appears to be a circular button. Let me try clicking at the very center of the visible coral circle.

Looking at the button more carefully, it appears the center might be around y=788-790. Let me try clicking at exactly (206, 788) which should be the center of the button.
The terminal shows "Prestidigitating..." which suggests some action is happening in the background. But the iPhone simulator still shows the same view with the mic button.

The button might require a different interaction. Let me try holding down the button (press and hold) instead of just tapping. According to the instructions, I can "hold and release" to create a task.

Actually, let me first try double-clicking on the mic button, or let me wait a moment and then try again.
I see the terminal is now showing my debug information. The Spoke app is still showing the same view with the Shopping filter active and the mic button at the bottom.

Let me try a different approach - I'll click the Simulator window first to make sure it's focused, then try to tap the mic button.
Good, I clicked in the middle of the iPhone screen and now the simulator should be focused. The screen still shows the Shopping filter view with 2 tasks visible and the coral mic button at the bottom.

Now let me try to tap the mic button. I'll click directly on the coral button at the bottom of the screen. The button appears to be centered horizontally and positioned near the bottom.

Let me look at the exact position more carefully - the mic button is a coral circle, and its center appears to be around (206, 788) based on the iPhone screen layout. Let me try clicking there.
The screen hasn't changed - the mic button is still showing in its default coral state. The click doesn't seem to be triggering the recording mode.

Let me try a slightly different approach. The instructions say I can "tap once to start, tap again to stop, or hold and release". Let me try holding down the mouse button on the mic button instead of just clicking.
I pressed down the left mouse button. Now I need to move it to the mic button position and hold it there, then release. Let me first move the cursor to the mic button location while holding the button down.

Wait, actually I need to release first and then do a proper hold action on the mic button. Let me release and try again.
Good, I released the mouse button. The simulator still shows the same view. Let me try a different approach - I'll click directly on the mic button but first move to the exact position and then do a single click.

Looking at the screen layout more carefully:
- The iPhone simulator frame starts at approximately x=35 and ends around x=390
- The iPhone screen content area is within that frame
- The mic button is a coral circular button at the bottom of the screen
- It appears centered horizontally at around x=206
- Looking at the button position, it appears to be around y=788 based on the visible button

Let me try clicking at a slightly different position - maybe the button is a bit lower. Let me try (206, 792).
feedback:
king at the very center of the visible coral circle.

Looking at the button more carefully, it appears the center might be around y=788-790. Let me try clicking at exactly (206, 788) which should be the center of the button.
The terminal shows "Prestidigitating..." which suggests some action is happening in the background. But the iPhone simulator still shows the same view with the mic button.

The button might require a different interaction. Let me try holding down the button (press and hold) instead of just tapping. According to the instructions, I can "hold and release" to create a task.

Actually, let me first try double-clicking on the mic button, or let me wait a moment and then try again.
I see the terminal is now showing my debug information. The Spoke app is still showing the same view with the Shopping filter active and the mic button at the bottom.

Let me try a different approach - I'll click the Simulator window first to make sure it's focused, then try to tap the mic button.
Good, I clicked in the middle of the iPhone screen and now the simulator should be focused. The screen still shows the Shopping filter view with 2 tasks visible and the coral mic button at the bottom.

Now let me try to tap the mic button. I'll click directly on the coral button at the bottom of the screen. The button appears to be centered horizontally and positioned near the bottom.

Let me look at the exact position more carefully - the mic button is a coral circle, and its center appears to be around (206, 788) based on the iPhone screen layout. Let me try clicking there.
The screen hasn't changed - the mic button is still showing in its default coral state. The click doesn't seem to be triggering the recording mode.

Let me try a slightly different approach. The instructions say I can "tap once to start, tap again to stop, or hold and release". Let me try holding down the mouse button on the mic button instead of just clicking.
I pressed down the left mouse button. Now I need to move it to the mic button position and hold it there, then release. Let me first move the cursor to the mic button location while holding the button down.

Wait, actually I need to release first and then do a proper hold action on the mic button. Let me release and try again.
Good, I released the mouse button. The simulator still shows the same view. Let me try a different approach - I'll click directly on the mic button but first move to the exact position and then do a single click.

Looking at the screen layout more carefully:
- The iPhone simulator frame starts at approximately x=35 and ends around x=390
- The iPhone screen content area is within that frame
- The mic button is a coral circular button at the bottom of the screen
- It appears centered horizontally at around x=206
- Looking at the button position, it appears to be around y=788 based on the visible button

Let me try clicking at a slightly different position - maybe the button is a bit lower. Let me try (206, 792).
